name: Slow Loop — 15 min lick focus
description: Isolate one hard lick or bar. Baseline a starting tempo. Run counts of 5-clean-in-a-row up the tempo ladder. Save your plateau for tomorrow.

items.label: Isolate the exact loop
items.instruction: Not the whole bar — the exact notes that fail. Often 4-8 notes; sometimes just 2 pitches with a hard interval. Write down what you're looping. This is the surgical target.

items.label: Baseline the starting tempo
items.instruction: Find the fastest tempo at which you can play the loop 9-10 clean out of 10 attempts. If you get 9-10 at your first tempo choice, you started too fast — go faster to find the ceiling. If you get 6-8, drop 20%. If <6, drop 30%. Play 10 attempts to confirm.

items.label: Count 1: 5 clean in a row at starting tempo
items.instruction: Loop at your starting tempo until you hit 5 CONSECUTIVE clean attempts. Flub on attempt 3? Restart the count at zero. Strictness is the whole method.

items.label: Nudge +5 BPM — count 2
items.instruction: Bump 5 BPM. Do another count of 5 clean in a row. If you can't hit 5 straight, drop back to the last tempo and stay there for another count.

items.label: Nudge +5 BPM — count 3
items.instruction: Another 5 BPM up. Another count of 5 clean.

items.label: Record your plateau
items.instruction: Whatever tempo you plateaued at (the last one where you hit 5 clean), write it down. Tomorrow's session starts 5 BPM below that. Do NOT play the loop at full tempo now to 'test' yourself — trust the slow work.
